concepts_list.name: rho_domain
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the momentum equation as a constant. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Density is a fundamental property of a fluid.

concepts_list.name: mu_domain
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the viscous stress tensor, representing the fluid's resistance to flow. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Viscosity is a property of a fluid.

concepts_list.name: t_domain
concepts_list.justification: Appears as the independent variable in the time derivative of velocity. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Time is the parameter of integration.

concepts_list.name: w_h_domain
concepts_list.description: Test function in the finite element approximation
concepts_list.justification: Used as the test function in the variational formulation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING:  A general test function is required in the PSPG method.

concepts_list.name: u_h_domain
concepts_list.justification: The approximate solution to the velocity equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Solution to the velocity equation.

concepts_list.name: p_h_domain
concepts_list.justification: The approximate solution to the pressure equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Solution to the pressure equation.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_N_domain
concepts_list.justification: Boundary of the domain where the prescribed traction is applied. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Specified boundary condition.

concepts_list.name: nabla_s_domain
concepts_list.justification: Used in calculating the dynamic viscosity. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: The term 'nabla_s' denotes the Sobolev gradient.

concepts_list.name: Omega_e
concepts_list.justification: Represents an individual element in the finite element mesh. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING:  A term commonly used in the context of finite element methods.

concepts_list.name: delta_e
concepts_list.justification: A parameter included in the pressure stabilization term. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING:  Term used in pressure stabilization schemes.

concepts_list.name: t_tilde_domain
concepts_list.justification: Represents a pseudo-traction term used for pressure stabilization. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING:  A pseudo-traction is used in stabilization techniques.

concepts_list.name: Omega
concepts_list.justification: The region in space over which the fluid flows. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: A general term used in fluid mechanics.

concepts_list.name: X_h
concepts_list.justification: The space of approximate solutions. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Finite element space definition.

new_concept_info.description:
Fluid density
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in the momentum equation as a constant. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Density is a fundamental property of a fluid.

Dynamic viscosity
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in the viscous stress tensor, representing the fluid's resistance to flow. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Viscosity is a property of a fluid.

Time
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as the independent variable in the time derivative of velocity. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Time is the parameter of integration.

Test function in the finite element approximation
JUSTIFICATION:
Used as the test function in the variational formulation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING:  A general test function is required in the PSPG method.

Approximate velocity field
JUSTIFICATION:
The approximate solution to the velocity equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Solution to the velocity equation.

Approximate pressure field
JUSTIFICATION:
The approximate solution to the pressure equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Solution to the pressure equation.

Boundary Gamma_N
JUSTIFICATION:
Boundary of the domain where the prescribed traction is applied. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Specified boundary condition.

Sobolev gradient operator
JUSTIFICATION:
Used in calculating the dynamic viscosity. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: The term 'nabla_s' denotes the Sobolev gradient.
